data.description:
█ OVERVIEW

Lorentzian Classification Premium represents the culmination of two years of collaborative development with over 1,000 beta testers from the TradingView community. Building upon the foundation of the open-source version, this premium edition introduces powerful enhancements that transform how machine-learning classification can be applied to market analysis.

The premium version maintains the core Lorentzian distance-based classification algorithm while expanding its capabilities …

0Z"></path></svg></span>23 070</div></div><div class="description-aqIxarm1"><div class="update-_PPhhqv6"><span class="ast-_PPhhqv6"><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>█ OVERVIEW</span></strong><br><br><span>Lorentzian Classification Premium represents the culmination of two years of collaborative development with over </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>1,000</span></strong><span> beta testers from the TradingView community. Building upon the foundation of the open-source version, this premium edition introduces powerful enhancements that transform how machine-learning classification can be applied to market analysis.</span><br><br><span>The premium version maintains the core Lorentzian distance-based classification algorithm while expanding its capabilities through triple the feature dimensionality (up to </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>15</span></strong><span> features), sophisticated mean-reversion detection, first-pullback identification, and a comprehensive signal taxonomy that goes far beyond simple buy/sell signals. Whether you're building automated trading systems, conducting deep market research, or integrating proprietary indicators into ML workflows, this tool provides the advanced edge needed for professional-grade analysis.</span><br><br><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>█ BACKGROUND</span></strong><br><br><span>Lorentzian Classification analyzes market structures, especially those exhibiting non-linear distortions under stress, by employing advanced distance metrics like the Lorentzian metric, prominent in fields such as relativity theory. Where traditional indicators assume flat space, we embrace the curve. The heart of this approach is the Lorentzian distance metric—a sophisticated mathematical tool. This framework adeptly navigates the complex curves and distortions of market space, aiming to provide insights that traditional analysis might miss, especially during moments of extreme volatility. It analyzes historical data from a multi-dimensional feature space consisting of various technical indicators of your choosing. Where traditional approaches fail, Lorentzian space reveals the true geometry of market dynamics.</span><br><br><a class="root-NFrHPBQH" href="https://www.tradingview.com/x/gjFrfcw8/" target="_blank" data-ast-type="image-node"><img class="image-NFrHPBQH" src="https://www.tradingview.com/x/gjFrfcw8/" alt="снимок" loading="lazy"></a><br><em><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Neighborhoods in Different Geometries:</span></strong><span> In the above figure, the Lorentzian metric creates distinctive cross-patterns aligned with feature axes (RSI, CCI, ADX), capturing both local similarity and dimensional extremes. This unique geometry allows the algorithm to recognize similar market conditions that Euclidean spheres and Manhattan diamonds would miss entirely. In LC Premium, users can have up to 15 features -- you are not limited to 3-dimensions. </span></em><br><br><span>Among the thousands of distance metrics discovered by mathematicians, each perceives data through its own geometric lens. The Lorentzian metric stands apart with its unique ability to capture market behavior during volatile events.</span><br><br><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>█ COMMUNITY-DRIVEN EVOLUTION</span></strong><br><br><span>It has been profoundly humbling over the past 2 years to witness this indicator's evolution through the collaborative efforts of our incredible community. This journey has been shaped by thousands of user suggestions and validated through real-world application.</span><br><br><span>A particularly amazing milestone was the development of a complete community-driven Python port, which meticulously matched even the most minute PineScript quirks. Building on this solid foundation, a new command-line interface (CLI) has opened up exciting possibilities for chart-specific parameter optimization:</span><br><a class="root-NFrHPBQH" href="https://www.tradingview.com/x/8TONE2oE/" target="_blank" data-ast-type="image-node"><img class="image-NFrHPBQH" src="https://www.tradingview.com/x/8TONE2oE/" alt="снимок" loading="lazy"></a><br><em><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Early insights from parameter optimization research:</span></strong><span> Through grid-search testing across thousands of parameter combinations, the analysis identifies which parameters have the biggest effects on performance and maps regions of stability across different market regimes. This reveals that optimal neighbor counts vary significantly based on market conditions—opening up incredible potential for timeframe-specific optimization.</span></em><br><br><span>This is just one of the insights gleaned so far from this ongoing investigation. The potential for chart-specific optimization for any given timeframe could transform how traders approach parameter selection.</span><br><br><span>Demand from power users for extra capabilities—while keeping the open-source version simple—sparked this Premium release. The open-source branch remains maintained, but the premium tier adds unique features for those who need an analytical edge and to leverage their own custom indicators as feature series for the algorithm.</span><br><br><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>█ KEY PREMIUM FEATURES</span></strong><br><br><br><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>📈 First Pullback Detection System</span></strong><br><ul class="root-vgdLRSQZ"><li><span>Automatically identifies high-probability trend-continuation entries after initial momentum moves.</span><br></li><li><span>Detects when price retraces to optimal entry zones following breakouts or trend initiations.</span><br></li><li><span>Green/red triangle signals often fire </span><em><span>before</span></em><span> main classification arrows.</span><br></li><li><span>Dedicated alerts for both bullish and bearish pullback opportunities.</span><br></li><li><span>Based on veryfid's extensive research into pullback mechanics and market structure.</span><br></li></ul><br><br><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>🔄 Dynamic Kernel Regression Envelope</span></strong><br><ul class="root-vgdLRSQZ"><li><span>Powerful, zero-setup confluence layer that immediately communicates trend shifts.</span><br></li><li><span>Dual-kernel system creates a visual envelope between trend estimates.</span><br></li><li><span>Color gradient dynamically represents prediction strength and market conviction.</span><br></li><li><span>Crossovers provide additional confirmation without cluttering your chart.</span><br></li><li><span>Professional visualization that rivals institutional-grade analysis tools.</span><br></li></ul><br><br><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>✨ Massively Expanded Dimensionality: 10 Custom Sources, 5 Built-In Sources </span></strong><br><ul class="root-vgdLRSQZ"><li><span>Transform the indicator from </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>5 built-in standard</span></strong><span> to </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>15 total</span></strong><span> total features—triple the analytical power.</span><br></li><li><span>Integrate </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>ANY</span></strong><span> TradingView indicator as a machine learning feature.</span><br></li><li><span>Built-in normalization ensures all indicators contribute equally regardless of scale.</span><br></li><li><span>Create theme-based systems: pure volume analysis, multi-timeframe momentum, or hybrid approaches.</span><br></li></ul><br><br><a class="root-NFrHPBQH" href="https://www.tradingview.com/x/sSHXNmDI/" target="_blank" data-ast-type="image-node"><img class="image-NFrHPBQH" src="https://www.tradingview.com/x/sSHXNmDI/" alt="снимок" loading="lazy"></a><br><br><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>📊 Tiered Mean Reversion Signals with Scalping Alerts</span></strong><br><ul class="root-vgdLRSQZ"><li><span>Regular (🔄) and Strong (⬇️/⬆️) mean reversion signals based on statistical extremes.</span><br></li><li><span>Opportunities often arise before candle close—perfect for scalping entries.</span><br></li><li><span>Visual markers appear at high-probability reversal zones.</span><br></li><li><span>Four specialized alert types: upward/downward for both regular and strong reversals.</span><br></li><li><span>Pre-optimized probability thresholds, no fine-tuning required.</span><br></li></ul><br><br><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>📅 Daily Kernel Trend Filter</span></strong><br><ul class="root-vgdLRSQZ"><li><span>Instantly cleans up noisy intraday charts by aligning with higher timeframe trends.</span><br></li><li><span>Swing traders report immediate signal quality improvement.</span><br></li><li><span>Automatically deactivates on daily+ timeframes (intelligent context awareness).</span><br></li><li><span>Reduces counter-trend signals by up to </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>60%</span></strong><span> on lower timeframes.</span><br></li><li><span>Simple toggle—no complex multi-timeframe setup required.</span><br></li></ul><br><br><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>📋 Professional Backtesting Stream (-6 to +6)</span></strong><br><ul class="root-vgdLRSQZ"><li><span>Multiple distinct signal types (including pullbacks, mean reversions, and kernel deviations) vs. basic binary (buy/sell) output for nuanced analysis.</span><br></li><li><span>Enables detailed walk-forward analysis and ML model discovery.</span><br></li><li><span>Remote fractals option extends analysis to deep historical patterns.</span><br></li><li><span>Reset factor control for fine-tuning neighbor diversity; Reset-factor tuning to control neighbor diversity.</span><br></li><li><span>Appeals to systematic traders exploring exotic data approaches.</span><br></li><li><span>Prevents temporal clustering bias in model training.</span><br></li></ul><br><br><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>█ HOW TO USE</span></strong><br><br><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Understanding the Approach (Core Concept):</span></strong><br><span>Lorentzian Classification uses a k-Nearest Neighbors (k-NN) algorithm. It searches for historical price action "neighborhoods" similar to the current market state. Instead of a simple straight-line (Euclidean) distance, it primarily uses a Lorentzian distance metric, which can account for market "warping" or distortions often seen during high volatility or significant events. Each historical neighbor "votes" on what happened next in its context, and these votes aggregate into a classification score for the current bar.</span><br><br><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Interpreting Bar Scores & Signals (Interpreting the Chart):</span></strong><br><ul class="root-vgdLRSQZ"><li><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Bar Prediction Values:</span></strong><span> Numbers over each candle (e.g., ranging from -8 to +8 if </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Neighbors Count</span></strong><span> is 8) represent the aggregated vote from the nearest neighbors. Strong positive scores (e.g., +7, +8) indicate a strong bullish consensus among historical analogs. Strong negative scores (e.g., -7, -8) indicate a strong bearish consensus. Scores near zero suggest neutrality or conflicting signals from neighbors. The intensity of bar colors (if </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Use Confidence Gradient</span></strong><span> is on) often reflects these scores.</span><br></li><li><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Main Arrows (Main Buy/Sell Labels):</span></strong><span> Large ▲/▼ labels are the primary entry signals generated when the overall classification (after filters) is bullish or bearish.</span><br></li><li><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Pullback Triangles:</span></strong><span> Small green/red ▲/▼ identify potential trend continuation entries. These signals often appear after an initial price move and a subsequent minor retracement, suggesting the trend might resume. This is based on recognizing patterns where a brief counter-movement is followed by a continued advance in the initial trend direction.</span><br></li><li><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Mean-Reversion Symbols:</span></strong><span> 🔄 (Regular Reversion) appears when price has crossed the average band of the Dynamic Kernel Regression Envelope. ⬇️/⬆️ (Strong Reversion) means price has crossed the far band of the envelope, indicating a more extreme deviation and potentially a stronger reversion opportunity.</span><br></li><li><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Custom Mean Reversion Deviation Markers (Deviation Dots):</span></strong><span> If </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Enable Custom Mean Reversion Alerts</span></strong><span> is on, these dots appear when price deviates from the main kernel regression line by a user-defined ATR multiple, signaling a custom-defined reversion opportunity.</span><br></li><li><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Kernel Regression Lines & Envelope:</span></strong><span> The Main Kernel Estimate (thicker line) is an adaptive moving average that smooths price and helps identify trend direction. Its color indicates the current trend bias. The Envelope (outer bands and a midline) creates a channel around price, and its interaction with price generates mean reversion signals.</span><br></li></ul><br><br><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Key Input Groups & Their Purpose:</span></strong><br><ul class="root-vgdLRSQZ"><li><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>🔧 GENERAL SETTINGS: </span></strong><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Reduce Price-Time Warping</span></strong><span>: Toggles the distance metric. When enabled, it reduces the characteristic "warping" effect of the default Lorentzian metric, making the distance calculation more Euclidean in nature. This may be suited for periods exhibiting less pronounced price-time distortions.</span><br><span>    </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Source</span></strong><span>: Price data for calculations (default: </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>close</span></strong><span>).</span><br><span>    </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Neighbors Count</span></strong><span>: The 'k' in k-NN – number of historical analogs considered.</span><br><span>    </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Max Bars Back</span></strong><span>: How far back the indicator looks for historical patterns.</span><br><span>    </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Show Exits / Use Dynamic Exits</span></strong><span>: Controls visibility and logic for exit signals.</span><br><span>    </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Include Full History (Use Remote Fractals)</span></strong><span>: Allows model to pick "exotic" fractals from deep chart history.</span><br><span>    </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Use Downsampling / Reset Factor</span></strong><span>: Advanced training parameters affecting neighbor selection.</span><br><span>    </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Show Trade Stats / Use Worst Case Estimates</span></strong><span>: Displays a real-time performance table (for calibration only).</span><br></li><li><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>🎛️ DEFINE CUSTOM SOURCES (OPTIONAL):</span></strong><br><span>    Integrate up to </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>10</span></strong><span> external data series (e.g., from other indicators) as features. Each can be optionally normalized. Load the external indicator on your chart first for it to appear in the dropdown.</span><br></li><li><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>🧠 FEATURE ENGINEERING:</span></strong><br><span>    Configure up to </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>15</span></strong><span> features for the k-NN algorithm. Select type (RSI, WT, CCI, ADX, Custom Sources), parameters, and enable/disable. Start simple (3-5 features) and add complexity gradually. Normalize features with vastly different scales.</span><br></li><li><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>🖥️ DISPLAY SETTINGS:</span></strong><br><span>    Controls visibility of chart elements: bar colors, prediction values/labels, envelope, etc.</span><br><span>    </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Align Signal with Current Bar</span></strong><span>: If true, pullback signals appear on the current bar (calculated on closed data). If false (default), they appear on the next bar.</span><br><span>    </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Use ATR Offset</span></strong><span>: Positions bar prediction values using ATR for visibility.</span><br></li><li><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>🧮 FILTERS SETTINGS:</span></strong><br><span>    Refine raw classification signals: Volatility, Regime, ADX, EMA/SMA, and Daily Kernel filters.</span><br></li><li><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>🌀 KERNEL SETTINGS (Main Kernel):</span></strong><br><span>    Adjust parameters for the primary Nadaraya-Watson Kernel Regression line. </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Lookback Window</span></strong><span>, </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Relative Weighting</span></strong><span>, </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Regression Level</span></strong><span>, </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Lag</span></strong><span> control sensitivity and smoothness.</span><br></li><li><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>✉️ ENVELOPE SETTINGS (for Mean Reversion):</span></strong><br><span>    Configure the dynamic Kernel Regression Envelope. </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>ATR Length</span></strong><span>, </span><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>Near/Far ATR Factor</span></strong><span> define band width.</span><br></li><li><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>🎨 COLOR SETTINGS (Colors):</span></strong><br><span>    Customize colors for all visual elements; override every palette element.</span><br></li></ul><br><br><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>General Approach to Using the Indicator (Suggested Workflow):</span></strong><br><ol class="root-vgdLRSQZ"><li><span>Load defaults and observe behavior: Familiarize yourself with the indicator's behavior.</span><br></li><li><span>Feature Engineering: Experiment with features, considering momentum, trend, and volatility. Add/replace features gradually.</span><br></li><li><span>Apply Filters: Refine signals according to your trading style.</span><br></li><li><span>Contextualize: Use kernels and envelope to understand broader trend and potential overbought/oversold areas.</span><br></li><li><span>Observe Signals: Pay attention to the interplay of main signals, pullbacks, and mean reversions. Watch interplay of main, pullback & mean-reversion signals.</span><br></li><li><span>Calibrate (Not Backtest): Use the "Trade Stats" table for real-time feedback on current settings. This is for calibration, *not a substitute for rigorous backtesting.*</span><br></li><li><span>Iterate & refine: Adjust settings, observe outcomes, and refine your approach.</span><br></li></ol><br><br><strong class="root-Tkn6WL2y"><span>█ ACKNOWLEDGMENTS</span></strong><br><span>This premium version wouldn't exist without the invaluable contributions of:</span><br><ul class="root-vgdLRSQZ"><li><span><a class="root-t974qRRv" href="https://www.tradingview.com/u/veryfid/" target="_self"><span class="text-t974qRRv">veryfid</span></a></span><span> for his groundbreaking ideas on unifying pullback detection with Lorentzian Classification, but most of all for always believing in and encouraging me and so many others. metadata.al:ios:app_name: TradingView - trading community, charts and quotes
metadata.description:
█ OVERVIEW

Lorentzian Classification Premium represents the culmination of two years of collaborative development with over 1,000 beta testers from the TradingView community. Building upon the foundation of the open-source version, this premium edition introduces powerful enhancements that transform how machine-learning classification can be applied to market analysis.

The premium version maintains the core Lorentzian distance-based classification algorithm while expanding its capabilities …
